subject: FAANG dev's guide to getting hired without a CS degree
snippet: At Facebook, I saw many candidates with prestigious degrees not make the cut. Here's how to stand out to hiring managers mo matter your background. Educative Mohamed, As someone who has interviewed
body:
At Facebook, I saw many candidates with prestigious degrees not make the cut. Here's how to stand out to hiring managers mo matter your background.

Educative (https://learn.educative.io/e3t/Ctc/RG+113/cz5H404/VX1_JZ2Sg--FW3qZVMR91WZPpW7vv0b_5r4tk2N7nzPwH3prCCW8wLKSR6lZ3ntVJZRs460V-1PW3_rV7S4Q9l1lW2hXbLS7nlKlDW4hFQrL1tKz8jW3Fxkjv3fNxWQW61g76T3mfm0jW268qWX68rJMfW4sssCp6Br61QMPDRvhvdN7KW7jV06_4ZhcJrW4RlK3G4Fs5KsW4mT3Mc9lH4qlN4T9ycklxNdCW73PCB98McHn_W4pz4_l3k8TbwW6Jvb-98kV_tPW3rlGZT6jbyGHVs5Ymb3vy5s3W6x0yPj12G600W512p_l1BNTwFW6KVb-V605l9xW84n9DF5Yw6ppN2lcs8L8_g2yW2RxQmS47Qz7xVw-p2w8gyvhnW1-BzwG5s-ym6W5bz-zs4Nm4TGW51hly87297Z_dSfjdW04 )

Mohamed,

As someone who has interviewed hundreds of developers over the years, I can tell you this:

Your hands-on experience, real-world projects, and problem-solving skills matter far more than the school you attended – or whether you attended one at all.

Of course, I’ve also seen candidates with CS degrees struggle to demonstrate how they applied their knowledge. The truth is, whether you learned through a degree program, boot camps, or self-study, what sets you apart is your ability to deliver results.

So, how do you stand out to recruiters and hiring managers in a competitive field?

Today, I'll share what I've learned from years of hiring engineers, including:

- Why experience often matters more than degrees
- 5 practical ways to highlight your skills (with or without a CS degree)
- How to prepare effectively for technical interviews

These strategies work for anyone looking to break into – or stand out in – tech.

👉 (Quick note: we're running a flash promo to help you start your learning on the right foot this year. Today you can grab 50% off our fully-loaded Premium Plus (https://learn.educative.io/e3t/Ctc/RG+113/cz5H404/VX1_JZ2Sg--FW3qZVMR91WZPpW7vv0b_5r4tk2N7nzPwH3prCCW8wLKSR6lZ3ntVJZRs460V-1PW3_rV7S4Q9l1lW2hXbLS7nlKlDW4hFQrL1tKz8jW3Fxkjv3fNxWQW61g76T3mfm0jW268qWX68rJMfW4sssCp6Br61QMPDRvhvdN7KW7jV06_4ZhcJrW4RlK3G4Fs5KsW4mT3Mc9lH4qlN4T9ycklxNdCW73PCB98McHn_W4pz4_l3k8TbwW6Jvb-98kV_tPW3rlGZT6jbyGHVs5Ymb3vy5s3W6x0yPj12G600W512p_l1BNTwFW6KVb-V605l9xW84n9DF5Yw6ppN2lcs8L8_g2yW2RxQmS47Qz7xVw-p2w8gyvhnW1-BzwG5s-ym6W5bz-zs4Nm4TGW51hly87297Z_dSfjdW04 ) plan, to get hands-on with the skills you need to level up).

Now let's dive in.

Recruiters prioritize experience over degrees. Here's why.

Once upon a time, earning a computer science degree was considered the only way to break into software engineering. But the industry has changed, and many developers now build thriving careers without formal CS training.

(In fact, according to StackOverflow (https://learn.educative.io/e3t/Ctc/RG+113/cz5H404/VX1_JZ2Sg--FW3qZVMR91WZPpW7vv0b_5r4tk2N7nzPw-3prCCW95jsWP6lZ3lXVMmW6k67sBnQW1rYGh585nYw2W5cDhYp1DR2VPW2PNbFZ1KQGYNN3XtsWfdPfwnW2Zbkmy1cvNp_W53PLvJ7DjxGMW7-2Dx37pHvtxW7DL3WP2FBj5kW6GNfT_4gkwqnW5_VBCJ3yFDWsW2snQzm1j3-QpW98Xwwy2B3_yvN81m7fKVGCn0W1YWTrz4PYxY2W5Dpmtb57VwLZW449JH91FqXqHVPf0kq7nnyF5W2XFWQ96vnfdCW7WBbX05pZBjbW4W13QJ4Vn_QCW8Y2vpV63shJ0W36qD0c8Qz2t3W1_s56G3pN3fyW751pW_1XWBngTsPKZ6wkrFNN7BCwsX9NYNfW5VNnjS22lSQJW4nxV8264QvJ2W3TTlc114XrGlf4Wl7ml04 ) , only 49% of professional developers learned to code at school.)

Here’s why the lack of a degree isn’t a dealbreaker:

- Your work speaks louder than your education: A GitHub repo full of contributions, a portfolio of completed projects, or impactful work at your current job tells a better story than a diploma.

- Hiring priorities have shifted: Companies like Google, Apple, and IBM prioritize skills, creativity, and problem-solving over credentials.

- Tech values lifelong learners: Software engineering evolves constantly, and recruiters know curious, adaptable developers thrive – no matter where they started.

If you’ve been coding, shipping, and solving problems, you already have what recruiters want. The key is communicating your experience effectively.

5 ways to position your experience and stand out as a candidate

Whether you’re new to the field or a seasoned engineer without a degree, here’s how to stand out.

1) Highlight impact over education

Your resume should focus on what you’ve achieved:

- “Redesigned X system, reducing downtime by 40%.”
- “Developed a tool that automated Y, saving Z hours weekly.”
- “Collaborated with a cross-functional team to launch a product that drove a 15% increase in customer retention.”

Show the outcomes of your work – not just your responsibilities.

2) Build a standout portfolio

A strong portfolio demonstrates your ability to deliver real-world results. Include:

- Personal projects: Showcase your creativity and initiative.
- Open-source contributions: Highlight your collaboration and problem-solving skills.
- Professional work: Break down large-scale projects: What was your role? What tools did you use? What impact did your work have?

Keep your portfolio clean, organized, and easy to navigate.

💡 Need project inspiration? Check out  301+ Projects from Educative. (https://learn.educative.io/e3t/Ctc/RG+113/cz5H404/VX1_JZ2Sg--FW3qZVMR91WZPpW7vv0b_5r4tk2N7nzPwH3prCCW8wLKSR6lZ3pVW72Npm94J0zvtW5pDllN3LMGxvW17Yys34Jd7WnW1NDZcB68ZJ-dW3BcPSj2gl-l3W1LwPLb2wq9mRW7sRFFn2-2gXdW18M4hJ4J7FKfW75x8pg16kcQ7W3FLTkB5HgP1gW2pchbT8cJB3TW5-khm28PkxnrW25rgj-5WYPXLW5rtCcr41gQYgN8HPqLvD3hlCW4CnVfS4qFcZ-W5B0Xdw8H_zphW748l5-4ZzSx5W4m38rf8jTtRyW4vrxXr2x2yd9W1r6cZy2n55HgN4cGpt5ySLRhW2qTLwc3SDk92W1XYmmM37wzTLW1BtQpC5w0rG1W4sSFhn68T3nKW40LNfy83zVHMW3ZMNrV4-ckfNf58cL_T04 )

3) Speak to transferable skills

If you’re transitioning from another field, emphasize how your previous experience applies to software engineering:

- Problem-solving: “Used data analysis skills to optimize inventory forecasting, reducing costs by 15%.”
- Communication: “Presented complex ideas to stakeholders, ensuring alignment across teams.”
- Project management: “Led cross-department initiatives, managing timelines and resources effectively.”

Draw clear connections between your skills and the value you’ll bring to a team.

4) Leverage your network

In tech, connections matter. Here’s how to build and use yours:

- Engage online: Share your work on GitHub, participate in Reddit discussions, or connect with peers on LinkedIn.

- Attend meetups or hackathons: These are great places to network with other developers and potential employers.

- Ask for referrals: Former colleagues, managers, or classmates can vouch for your skills and work ethic.

5) Prepare well for interviews

Even the perfect resume and portfolio won’t land the job if you can’t back it up in the interview. This is where you truly stand out ... or fall short.

Recruiters and hiring managers aren’t just testing what you know – they want to see how you think, solve problems, and communicate.

Some tips to help you prepare effectively so you can shine when it counts:

- Practice common technical questions: System design, algorithms, and debugging challenges are interview staples. Familiarize yourself with these types of problems.

- Simulate real-world scenarios: Be ready to walk through examples of how you’ve applied your skills to solve problems or deliver results in past projects.

- Refine your communication skills: Clearly explain your thought process, the trade-offs you considered, and why you made specific decisions.

- Leverage mock interviews: Tools like Educative’s Interview Prep courses can help you practice in a low-stakes environment.

- --> If mock interviews are hard to come by, tools like AI Mock Interviews (https://learn.educative.io/e3t/Ctc/RG+113/cz5H404/VX1_JZ2Sg--FW3qZVMR91WZPpW7vv0b_5r4tk2N7nzPw-3prCCW95jsWP6lZ3pXW8bqKh81SL2bTW4WZZpk1-krXGW9bM92H3Kyn-nW2lj4-c2nz6JXW6CQ2F18VNzcFW7s4-G36KwxLMW1XMXY37Dr7DCW825MqT6Wy4V8W2w5ZxV3YW1z5W8RbGXC1PjRMMW4VZjwr4R-pynW3c5Nvg4sRDglW7dYjJq6C-z98W7gwDWz8p3b-CMnBxxcR2g9pW2NSQpL31wSBKN4TnJf6_8mSdN5RPFP4NG3hwW7hMYh32Hch8KW2TtZzL43ZHMfW5wBvh07Bm6-HW7Ssr-V211gn9W1-nQcP6tTDTbW94Rrb08XFy_yW4kw4ZR1gZkXRW2Gpr9P1Kh0hSV879sk7wp-M9VDqyKb4jLhf7VN3WcN5BVMWLW5TLz1c5RbWZlf7bN1zW04 ) can help you practice ahead of the real thing.

Want even more tailored support? Personalized Adaptive Learning (https://learn.educative.io/e3t/Ctc/RG+113/cz5H404/VX1_JZ2Sg--FW3qZVMR91WZPpW7vv0b_5r4tk2N7nzPwH3prCCW8wLKSR6lZ3kxW2RpZkH5Sw2PsW1csX0b6qQwpwW7rgZWz29DYK_W7Sx5rP3Xg9whW8hj_2N39_wXvW339YTb8m0gsnW3LKCw32nRjd7N8Xmg1bYTN01VWN7kl2Sh3NdW8dG4zS78Nm0nW2g7dbK21cw0GW8-bHFk37nZkrW6bj7-F96BlGgW5QNMnG37TCL9W1hGTg42gr2pNW90BNBT4m-4rRMCKvyTsdMBLW1jFjML6CBygSW4mSJgY6Ngb1SW3M2t1d2YQnPyW5-1j0_85l6DqW1YrrnV33xpjWN10fRP7K8bhPW8W4Nrk3rWbBbN8XrDspwfl_wW6jZHcN3-y1G1W3ZkNys31T9qYW4ym6FS4TdkJ9f48qtbj04 ) can create a custom interview prep plan based on your target role, company, and timeline.

Your experience is your most powerful asset

Here’s what I’ve learned as someone who’s been on both sides of the hiring table: Recruiters care about results.

They’re not interested in how you learned to code – they’re interested in what you’ve built and the value you can bring to their team.

If you’ve been coding, shipping, and solving problems, you already have everything you need to stand out. It’s not about compensating for not having a degree ... it’s about showcasing the skills, creativity, and impact you already bring to the table.

If you're ready to stand out, take these actions today:

- Polish your resume to highlight your impact.
- Refine your portfolio with projects that showcase your skills.
- Prepare for interviews to confidently explain your experience and problem-solving approach.

And if you're looking for more ways to level up, Educative has the tools, courses, and resources to help you succeed.

You’ve got this.

Happy learning!

Fahim ul Haq

CEO & Co-founder

educative.io (https://learn.educative.io/e3t/Ctc/RG+113/cz5H404/VX1_JZ2Sg--FW3qZVMR91WZPpW7vv0b_5r4tk2N7nzPwH3prCCW8wLKSR6lZ3ntVJZRs460V-1PW3_rV7S4Q9l1lW2hXbLS7nlKlDW4hFQrL1tKz8jW3Fxkjv3fNxWQW61g76T3mfm0jW268qWX68rJMfW4sssCp6Br61QMPDRvhvdN7KW7jV06_4ZhcJrW4RlK3G4Fs5KsW4mT3Mc9lH4qlN4T9ycklxNdCW73PCB98McHn_W4pz4_l3k8TbwW6Jvb-98kV_tPW3rlGZT6jbyGHVs5Ymb3vy5s3W6x0yPj12G600W512p_l1BNTwFW6KVb-V605l9xW84n9DF5Yw6ppN2lcs8L8_g2yW2RxQmS47Qz7xVw-p2w8gyvhnW1-BzwG5s-ym6W5bz-zs4Nm4TGW51hly87297Z_dSfjdW04 )

body:
AI is reshaping the future, are you ready to make it work for you and your career?


As a Premium subscriber, you’re invited to an exclusive live conversation with LinkedIn co-founder and tech pioneer Reid Hoffman. He’ll explore how to use AI to supercharge your career by answering your questions live and sharing key insights from his new book, Superagency: What Could Possibly Go Right with Our AI Future.


﻿Click 'Attend' to Join the Conversation﻿

____________________________________________



Here’s what you’ll take away:

  • A practical roadmap for integrating AI tools to get ahead at work 
  • Actionable strategies for using AI to level-up your decision-making, creativity and productivity
  • Ideas and tips for job-hunting and cultivating amazing career opportunities in the age of AI 


﻿Click 'Attend' to Join the Conversation﻿


Can’t join us live? No problem. 

Register via the above link to access the replay.

subject: Centari application update
snippet: Hi Mohamed Thank you for taking the time to chat with me. After careful consideration, the team has decided not to move forward with the application process. As a small team, we have to make tough
body:
Hi Mohamed
Thank you for taking the time to chat with me. After careful consideration,
the team has decided not to move forward with the application process.
As a small team, we have to make tough calls with limited information and
be very selective about who we move forward with. This wasn’t an easy
decision, and it’s more about our current priorities than a reflection on
you.
Thanks again for considering us.
Wishing you all the best,

body:
View this email in your browser (https://mailchi.mp/idrf.ca/gaza-ceasefire-plan-update?e=1ec704905a)
https://donate.idrf.ca/site/Donation2;jsessionid=00000000.app30133a?df_id=1822&mfc_pref=T&1822.donation=form1&NONCE_TOKEN=DF7357117D238A198191D95BA3099DD2&set.DonationLevel=2398&utm_medium=email&utm_source=email&utm_campaign=Gaza-Ceasefire-Update-Donate&utm_source=IDRF+Master+List&utm_campaign=193271f287-Gaza_Ceasefire_Update_01_20_25&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_ab6dbcc7b1-193271f287-109041042&mc_cid=193271f287&mc_eid=UNIQID

Right now, IDRF is rushing aid to Gaza.

As of January 19, the first day of the ceasefire, over 600 aid trucks entered the Gaza strip, a much-needed increase from the previous daily average — only 40 trucks per day.

While IDRF has been active in Gaza throughout the last 15 months, we hope to scale our response dramatically in the coming weeks.
Send Aid to Gaza (https://donate.idrf.ca/site/Donation2;jsessionid=00000000.app30133a?df_id=1822&mfc_pref=T&1822.donation=form1&NONCE_TOKEN=DF7357117D238A198191D95BA3099DD2&set.DonationLevel=2398&utm_medium=email&utm_source=email&utm_campaign=Gaza-Ceasefire-Update-Donate&utm_source=IDRF+Master+List&utm_campaign=193271f287-Gaza_Ceasefire_Update_01_20_25&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_ab6dbcc7b1-193271f287-109041042&mc_cid=193271f287&mc_eid=UNIQID)
Thus far, in 2025, IDRF has:

✅ Delivered over 200,000 litres of water, daily, across Gaza
✅ Provided over 2,000 hot meals, daily
✅ Continued operating 5 medical clinics across Gaza
✅ Conducted psychosocial support sessions for children
✅ Provided shelter reinforcements in the North
✅ Provided food vouchers to families

Our work continues — but this is nowhere near enough.

As the dust settles, the people of Gaza have been left with no shelter, no food, no water, no fuel — the healthcare system is in ruins, and homes have been reduced to rubble.

Our duty does not end with a ceasefire.

Every donation, no matter how small, is helping us rush aid deliveries to Gaza. Thank you for standing with us.

body:
Hi Mohamed,

Thank you for your interest in our Software Engineer (Growth) position here at Alchemy. We received many applications from qualified candidates and appreciate the time and effort you took to apply.

After careful consideration, we regret to inform you that we have decided not to move forward with your application at this time. While your background is impressive, we have identified other candidates whose skills and experience more closely match the requirements of the position.

We sincerely appreciate your interest and hope that you will keep us in mind for future opportunities.

We also encourage you to join our curated web3 talent collective ( https://alchemy.pallet.com/talent/welcome?pallet=&step=welcome&application=true ), where top projects across L1s, L2s, NFT platforms, DeFi projects, and more are actively hiring and would love to speak with you.
